title: Completing your Australian tax return
description: Follow these instructions to complete myTax to ensure that your tax return is filled out correctly and prevent delays with your assessment.

rows: On Personalise return screen, select the following: 'You received salary, wages or other income on a payment summary, or Australian Government payments', followed by 'Foreign employment income (on a payment summary)'. Click Next.
On Prepare return screen, alongside 'Payment summaries' section click 'Add/Edit'.
In 'Payment summaries' section, alongside 'Foreign employment income', click 'Add'.

Show the following details: Payer's name Payer's ABN (if unknown, leave blank) 'Joint Petroleum Development Area (JPDA)' as Foreign employment income type Residency status Any other details from your PAYG payment summary – foreign employment Note : You may need to adjust some amounts from your PAYG payment summary – foreign employment in myTax. To see if this applies to you, go to Step 5. myTax will use your answers to complete the schedule of additional information for you.

If the Residency status you selected is: 'A - Australian resident for the whole year', go to Part 1 – Australian Resident . 'B - Timor-Leste resident for the whole year', go to Part 2 – Resident of Timor-Leste . 'C - Resident of another country for the whole year', go to Part 3 – Resident of another country . 'D - Changed residence during the year', you may need to apportion your JPDA income and complete more than one part. You will need to follow the instructions in Part 1 for the period that you were an Australian resident Part 2 for the period that you were a resident of Timor-Leste Part 3 for the period that you were a resident of another country.

content_text:
Step 1 | On Personalise return screen, select the following: 'You received salary, wages or other income on a payment summary, or Australian Government payments', followed by 'Foreign employment income (on a payment summary)'. Click Next.
Step 2 | On Prepare return screen, alongside 'Payment summaries' section click 'Add/Edit'.
Step 3 | In 'Payment summaries' section, alongside 'Foreign employment income', click 'Add'.
Step 4 | Show the following details: Payer's name Payer's ABN (if unknown, leave blank) 'Joint Petroleum Development Area (JPDA)' as Foreign employment income type Residency status Any other details from your PAYG payment summary – foreign employment Note : You may need to adjust some amounts from your PAYG payment summary – foreign employment in myTax. To see if this applies to you, go to Step 5. myTax will use your answers to complete the schedule of additional information for you.
Step 5 | If the Residency status you selected is: 'A - Australian resident for the whole year', go to Part 1 – Australian Resident . 'B - Timor-Leste resident for the whole year', go to Part 2 – Resident of Timor-Leste . 'C - Resident of another country for the whole year', go to Part 3 – Resident of another country . 'D - Changed residence during the year', you may need to apportion your JPDA income and complete more than one part. You will need to follow the instructions in Part 1 for the period that you were an Australian resident Part 2 for the period that you were a resident of Timor-Leste Part 3 for the period that you were a resident of another country.

child_links.title: Part 3 – Resident of another country
child_links.description: Use this part if you were a resident of a country other than Australia or Timor-Leste for tax purposes during 2016–17.
